rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Training platform = Claude Code (CLI + Desktop) + Cowork.
Verify capabilities BEFORE asserting, not after.
Maintain living capability notes; consult before asserting.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Maintainer block line ~158 says '4-task case not yet live-tested but plausible by extension' — per Antti's 2026-04-29 live confirmation, 4-task Cowork IS confirmed. Maintainer note is stale but student-facing body ('up to four Cowork tasks') is accurate. Update the maintainer note to reflect confirmed status.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Practitioner observation beats docs lookup.
Known correct Claude Code facts.
Sessions are the unit of work.
No premature infrastructure.
No as-is imports from the internet.
Source URL + type label — mandatory.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Maintainer block line ~149 references 'Boris Cherny / Anthropic observation' with '[SOURCE NEEDED]' — honest label. No unattributed factual claims in student-facing body. Maintainer-block citation debt is tracked; needs resolution before first cohort per check_research_claims rule 11.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Freshness rule — 6 months.
Zombie stat guard.
Source-verification maintainer block on files with practitioner citations.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The Boris Cherny / Anthropic 'filesystem is the meeting room' claim in the maintainer block has '[SOURCE NEEDED]' — citation debt is visible and labeled. A formal source-verification section (per rule 11) is not present but neither is a shipped practitioner citation — the [SOURCE NEEDED] marker defers until the URL is pinned. No blocking on draft.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No placeholders inside fenced blocks.
Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label with destination, blank line, fenced block.
Installed Claude Code Skills invoke by name, never by path.
Chain prompts by back-reference and deterministic path.
Markdown shout inside fenced prompt blocks is dead weight.
Runtime-fork prompt blocks: paired divs, shared chip.
Artifact destinations are explicit subpaths.
Artifact-realism check.
Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
Verification machinery needs a smoke-test before being trusted.
Context-loading tiers as staged progression.
Delegation boundaries — student brings, Claude screens.
Load-bearing platform feature gets no fallback — gate or redesign.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body at line ~19 says 'Three retrievers is the default; if you can't get one running today... skip it and proceed with what you have. The lesson works with two retrievers, even one.' This is a graceful-degradation carve-out for connector availability (wiki/docs/internet), not a fallback for the multi-session platform feature itself. The concurrent-session mechanic (the load-bearing feature) has no fallback offered. The connector fallback is appropriate per check_pedagogy #17 (corporate infra is nice-to-have). Verdict: PASS.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Open your training directory in up to four Claude Code sessions.
Open up to four Cowork tasks on this connected folder.
Desktop users: open three app sessions alongside the existing one. CLI users: three more terminal sessions, each running `claude` from the training directory.
Run the searches through Claude's connector to my wiki.
Pull the content via the doc-store connector (OneDrive, SharePoint, Google Drive, whichever I use)
In Claude Desktop's Cowork tab, start three more tasks pointed at your training directory

citations_checked.claim: filesystem is the meeting room — Boris Cherny / Anthropic observation
